user_query:
Read the feature spec and implementation plan under input/spec.md and input/plan.md. Deliver a small, production-ready Python package using a two-phase implement-then-audit loop with three parallel review passes (simplify, harden, spec). Run at most 3 audit rounds with fresh, read-only auditors each round. Exit when: clean audit (zero findings), or low-only findings (fix inline and exit), or loop cap reached with all critical/high fixed.

Scope and deliverables:
1) Implement a minimal package per the spec with these files under output/:
   - output/src/text_reports/__init__.py
   - output/src/text_reports/report.py (functions: parse_csv(path: str) -> list[dict], compute_metrics(rows: list[dict]) -> dict, render_markdown(metrics: dict) -> str; include basic input validation and error handling)
   - output/src/text_reports/cli.py (CLI that accepts an --input CSV path and prints a markdown summary to stdout; include a usage/help entrypoint)
   - output/tests/test_report.py (unit tests covering the functions and a basic CLI help check)
   - output/README.md (how to compile/type-check if applicable and run tests; include exact commands or steps you used)
   - output/modified_files.json (JSON array of all files you created/modified, paths relative to output/)
2) Perform the audit loop with three distinct, read-only review passes each round: simplify (clarity/complexity), harden (security/resilience), and spec (conformance to spec/acceptance criteria). Pass the explicit modified file list to each auditor and scope their review to only those files.
3) Produce auditor logs for at least the first round under output/audit/ with one file per auditor: output/audit/round1_simplify.md, output/audit/round1_harden.md, output/audit/round1_spec.md. Each log must include: a "Files to review:" section listing the modified files; at least one finding format section with the fields "File and line number", "Category", "What's wrong", "Severity" (and for harden also an "Attack vector" field if applicable) and a concrete "Fix recommendation"; and an "Out-of-scope observations" section (can be empty). If you run additional rounds, add round2_*.md and/or round3_*.md similarly.
4) Document pass: after fixes in a non-initial round, add up to 5 short inline comments across files you touched to explain non-obvious decisions from the original implementation (do not comment on the audit fixes themselves).
5) Final hardening summary: write output/report.md containing these sections and fields exactly: a top-level heading "Hardening Summary"; "Audit rounds completed: X of 3 max"; "Exit reason:" with one of [Clean audit, Low-only round, Loop cap reached]; "Findings by round" with per-auditor counts and severities; "Actions taken" (what you fixed and what you skipped with reasons and any document pass notes); "Unresolved" (if any, with reasons); and "Out-of-scope observations". Also include a short "Budget" section stating the cumulative diff growth estimate and confirming you stayed within the <=30% budget cap for fix rounds.

Quality gates (non-negotiable):
- Do not include any TODO or FIXME comments anywhere in output/src or output/tests.
- Each Python source file must start with a module docstring and include at least one explicit raise for invalid input (e.g., ValueError) where appropriate.
- The auditor logs must show that auditors were given a bounded scope (the modified files list) and did not claim to fix code; they only report findings.
- The final summary must match the required structure and clearly justify the exit condition.

Write all artifacts only under output/ as specified.
